title: camelina dentata
intro: Camelina dentata, also known as false flax or gold-of-pleasure, is an annual flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. Consuming camelina oil or using the plant's herbal preparations may provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Camelina oil can be extracted through cold pressing or solvent extraction, and it is often used in skincare products, cosmetics, and food supplements.
